title: pittosporum pullifolium
intro: Pittosporum pullifolium, also known as the Forster's cheesewood, is a flowering plant native to Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum pullifolium include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
